query: SELECT * FROM events ORDER BY created_at DESC;
context: Show the most recent events on an admin dashboard.

ground_truth_issues.description: SELECT * pulls a wide payload when the dashboard only needs a few columns.
ground_truth_issues.fix: SELECT id, event_name, created_at FROM events ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 50;

ground_truth_issues.description: The dashboard query is missing a LIMIT and can scan far more rows than necessary.
ground_truth_issues.fix: SELECT id, event_name, created_at FROM events ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 50;

query: SELECT c.id, c.name, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM orders o WHERE o.customer_id = c.id) AS order_count FROM customers c;
context: Show each customer with the number of orders they have placed.

ground_truth_issues.description: The correlated subquery re-counts orders per row and should be rewritten as a join with GROUP BY.
ground_truth_issues.fix: SELECT c.id, c.name, COUNT(o.id) AS order_count FROM customers c LEFT JOIN orders o ON o.customer_id = c.id GROUP BY c.id, c.name;

query: SELECT DISTINCT email FROM users WHERE email IS NOT NULL;
context: Export non-null user emails for a CRM sync.

ground_truth_issues.description: DISTINCT is redundant because users.email is already unique.
ground_truth_issues.fix: SELECT email FROM users WHERE email IS NOT NULL;

query: SELECT o.id, o.total, u.name FROM orders o JOIN users u ON u.id = o.user_id WHERE DATE(o.created_at) = '2026-04-10';
context: List orders placed on a specific date with the user name attached.

ground_truth_issues.description: Wrapping created_at with DATE() prevents efficient use of the created_at index.
ground_truth_issues.fix: SELECT o.id, o.total, u.name FROM orders o JOIN users u ON u.id = o.user_id WHERE o.created_at >= '2026-04-10' AND o.created_at < '2026-04-11';

query: SELECT id, name FROM products WHERE LOWER(name) LIKE '%pro%';
context: Search products whose names contain the text pro.

ground_truth_issues.description: Applying LOWER(name) on every row prevents the index on name from being used efficiently.
ground_truth_issues.fix: SELECT id, name FROM products WHERE name ILIKE 'pro%';

ground_truth_issues.description: The leading wildcard in LIKE '%pro%' forces a full scan instead of an index-friendly prefix lookup.
ground_truth_issues.fix: SELECT id, name FROM products WHERE name ILIKE 'pro%';

context: Find all events that happened on a specific date.

ground_truth_issues.description: Using DATE() function on an indexed column prevents index usage. Use a range comparison instead.
ground_truth_issues.fix: SELECT * FROM events WHERE created_at >= '2024-01-15 00:00:00' AND created_at < '2024-01-16 00:00:00';

ground_truth_issues.description: SELECT * returns all columns when only specific fields may be needed.
ground_truth_issues.fix: SELECT id, name, created_at FROM events WHERE created_at >= '2024-01-15' AND created_at < '2024-01-16';

query: SELECT * FROM products ORDER BY RAND() LIMIT 10;
context: Show 10 random products on the homepage.

ground_truth_issues.description: ORDER BY RAND() generates a random value for every row in the table, causing a full table scan and sort. Extremely slow on large tables.
ground_truth_issues.fix: SELECT * FROM products WHERE id >= (SELECT FLOOR(RAND() * (SELECT MAX(id) FROM products))) LIMIT 10;
